note: Annual spending areas used to give scale to debt and debt interest. Comparisons help readers picture size only; they do not imply budgets could be redirected to or from debt. Scope varies by area (see each note) — figures are estimates pending line-by-line verification against HM Treasury statistics.

areas.name: NHS / health
areas.notes: Total Department of Health and Social Care budget for England. The NHS England core budget alone is about £152bn.

areas.name: Education
areas.source_name: HM Treasury PESA / IFS education spending report
areas.notes: Total UK public education spending. The DfE day-to-day budget for England alone is about £101bn.

areas.name: Defence
areas.source_name: HM Treasury Spending Review 2025 / House of Commons Library (CBP-8175)
areas.notes: The MoD departmental budget for 2025/26 (about 2% of GDP). On the broader NATO definition, which adds items such as pensions and security funds, UK spending is about 2.4% of GDP; the government plans to reach 2.5% by 2027 and 3.5% by 2035.

areas.name: State pension
areas.source_name: DWP / HM Treasury benefit expenditure tables

areas.name: Welfare and social protection (total)
areas.source_name: DWP benefit expenditure tables / HM Treasury
areas.notes: Total social security spending (DWP benefits, tax credits, Child Benefit). Includes the state pension shown separately above. The broader social-protection function is about £379bn.

areas.name: Total tax receipts
areas.notes: Total tax receipts, about 36% of GDP. Total public sector current receipts (including non-tax income) are higher, around £1,240bn.
